title: Aegis Constitution
description: The foundational identity and context of a project. This is the first file any agent reads upon entering the workspace. It answers: What is this place, and what matters here? The required fields define the universal skeleton every tool can rely on. Additional fields are permitted — the CLI and LLM may surface domain-specific context (sensitivity tiers, domain maps, cross-domain rules) that enriches governance beyond the baseline.

$schema.description: Reference to the Aegis constitution schema for validation.

version.description: Aegis schema version this file conforms to.

project.description: Core identity of the project. Required fields form the universal skeleton. Additional fields (e.g. domains, sensitivity_tiers, cross_domain_rules) are permitted for domain-specific governance.

purpose.description: A concise statement of what this project is and why it exists. One to three sentences maximum. This anchors every decision an agent makes.
examples: A B2B SaaS platform for inventory management, serving mid-size retail companies. Speed and reliability are prioritized over feature breadth.

architecture.description: The high-level architectural pattern of the project.

module_map.description: Top-level modules or packages in the project, with their purpose and ownership boundaries. This is the organizational chart of the codebase.

purpose.description: What this module does, in one sentence.

owner.description: The role name (from roles/) that has primary responsibility for this module. If unset, governed by default role.

internal_dependencies.description: Other module paths this module depends on.

required_artifacts.description: Files that must exist in the repository. Agents should create these if they are missing. These are project-level deliverables — documentation, licensing, contribution guides — that define the project's public identity and operational standards.

purpose.description: What this file is for and what it should contain, in one sentence.

source.description: Where the content should be derived from, if applicable.

tech_stack.description: The technologies in use. Agents use this to calibrate their suggestions and avoid recommending incompatible tools.

languages.description: Programming languages used in this project.

package_managers.description: Package managers used across the project.

key_libraries.description: Important libraries that agents should know about and prefer.

purpose.description: What this library is used for.

scope.description: Which part of the codebase uses this library.

principles.description: The non-negotiable values and priorities of this project. These are the 'why behind the rules.' When an agent faces an ambiguous decision, principles are the tiebreaker.

statement.description: The principle expressed as a clear directive.
examples: Reliability is more important than speed of delivery. Never ship something that is not thoroughly tested.

priority.description: Relative priority. 1 is highest. When principles conflict, lower number wins.

build_commands.description: Essential commands agents need to build, test, and validate the project.

custom.description: Any additional commands agents should know about.
